title: Multi-Instance and Sentinel Migration Specification with Identity Continuity and Re-enrollment
description: Define the mechanisms that enable: (a) multiple Sentinel instances for the same service (load distribution / HA), (b) VM/host migration without identity change, (c) re-registration/rejoin with the same DID and credentials, (d) controlled key rotation with rolling window. This is the specification that governs TASK-039, TASK-048, and TASK-049.
objectives: Horizontal scaling without changing the exposed DID (DID remains constant)
VM/host migration with the same DID via shared secret storage (Vault/KMS) or encrypted backup
Safe re-enrollment after incident or key rotation

implementation_guidance.overview: The core model is: one logical DID per (service_id, role, env) tuple. This DID is shared across all instances of the same service/role/env. Instances are differentiated by an ephemeral instance_id (UUID) used only for observability and heartbeat tracking. The DID private key is stored in a shared secret backend (Vault/KMS for prod, encrypted local file for dev).
steps: Step 1 - Identity Model: Define the DID as scoped to (service_id, role, env). All instances of 'citizen-data-service / PRODUCER / prod' share the same DID and the same private key (accessed via Vault). Instance_id is a UUID4 generated at container/process startup, stored only in memory and in heartbeat records — never in the DID document or credentials.

Step 2 - Key Backend: Two backend modes. MODE_VAULT (production): Ed25519 private key stored as Vault KV secret at path sentinels/{service_id}/{role}/{env}/did_private_key. Access via AppRole or Kubernetes auth. Key is read at startup and cached in-memory (not written to disk). MODE_LOCAL (development/single-host): Ed25519 private key stored in an AES-256-GCM encrypted JSON file at path {data_dir}/keys/{service_id}-{role}-{env}.key.enc. Master key derived from ENV_SECRET_KEY environment variable using scrypt (N=32768, r=8, p=1).

Step 3 - Instance Registration (Rejoin): When a new instance starts, it: (1) Reads the DID private key from the backend. (2) Derives the DID from the public key. (3) Calls Discovery /api/v1/sentinels/rejoin with: {did, instance_id, service_id, role, env, endpoint_url, proof: <PoP over challenge>}. (4) Discovery verifies PoP matches the existing sentinel DID record. (5) Discovery updates sentinel_instances table: upsert {sentinel_id, instance_id, endpoint_url, last_seen=now, status=ACTIVE}. (6) Instance begins heartbeat loop.

Step 4 - Multi-Instance Endpoint Publication: Each instance publishes its own endpoint_url. The ServiceDescriptor (TASK-032) contains an endpoints array. When a new instance connects: Discovery updates the ServiceDescriptor's endpoint list. When an instance disconnects/decommissions: Discovery removes its endpoint from the list after TTL expiry.

Step 5 - Key Rotation Procedure: (1) Pre-rotation: all instances are running with old key K1. (2) Admin triggers rotation via Discovery API POST /api/v1/sentinels/{id}/rotate-key. (3) Discovery generates new key K2, stores in Vault as current version, keeps K1 as previous version. (4) Discovery sets sentinel state = KEY_ROTATING with grace_period_end = now + grace_seconds (default 600s). (5) During grace period: Producer Sentinels accept VPs signed with EITHER K1 or K2 (verify kid in JWS header). (6) Discovery re-issues all credentials (VCs) with K2 as the new subject key. (7) Instances running with K1 fetch updated config bundle containing K2 (TASK-040) and hot-reload keys. (8) After grace_period_end: K1 is rejected, Discovery revokes old VCs.

Step 6 - VM Migration Procedure (Single Instance): (1) Admin creates migration_ticket (TASK-005) via discovery UI. (2) Operator copies to new host (out-of-band secure channel). (3) In MODE_VAULT: new host just reads key from Vault — no file transfer needed. (4) In MODE_LOCAL: operator copies the encrypted key file to new host using secure file transfer (not email, not plain HTTP). (5) New Sentinel runs onboarding/rejoin using migration_ticket. (6) Old Sentinel is decommissioned (TASK-033).

Step 7 - Encrypted Backup (MODE_LOCAL dev only): sentinelctl backup --passphrase produces a file {sentinel_id}.wallet.bak. Contents: AES-256-GCM encrypted JSON containing {service_id, role, env, did, private_key_hex, vc_metadata (no VC payloads), key_version}. Master key = scrypt(passphrase, salt=random_16_bytes). File includes checksum (HMAC-SHA256). sentinelctl restore --file --passphrase loads and verifies checksum before restoring.

Step 8 - Graceful Shutdown: Before process termination, instance sends DELETE /api/v1/sentinels/instances/{instance_id} or marks itself DRAINING via heartbeat status. Discovery removes its endpoint from the ServiceDescriptor immediately. Kubernetes preStop hook should call sentinelctl drain --timeout=30s.

files: libs/common/secret_storage/key_backend.py — abstraction for Vault/local key storage

rejoin.description: Register a new instance of an existing Sentinel

request_body.endpoint_url: string — HTTPS URL this instance listens on
request_body.proof: string(JWT) — PoP proof signed with DID key

fields: sentinel_id: UUID — FK to sentinels table

security_constraints: Shared DID key in Vault MUST use Vault namespacing: separate path per (service_id, role, env) — no sharing across services
Encrypted backup in MODE_LOCAL is for break-glass only — operators must be warned that this is a last-resort mechanism
Grace period during key rotation MUST be enforced: after grace_period_end, old key MUST be rejected with no exceptions
The instance_id MUST NOT appear in any signed artifact (VCs, ProofClaims) — it is purely an operational identifier
Migration ticket MUST be single-use and have a maximum TTL of 30 minutes

technical_requirements: HashiCorp Vault KV v2 API for production key storage
AES-256-GCM for local key file encryption (Python cryptography library)
scrypt for password-based key derivation (N=32768, r=8, p=1)
PostgreSQL for sentinel_instances table with last_seen tracking
Redis for grace-period key version cache during rotation
acceptance_criteria: Multi-instance specification document exists with complete identity model
Vault and local key backends are defined with exact paths and access patterns
Key rotation procedure has enumerated steps with grace window logic documented
Grace period: both old and new keys are accepted during the window; old key is rejected after
Encrypted backup produces a file that can restore the sentinel DID identity identically
Rejoin endpoint is idempotent: calling it twice with the same instance_id returns the same response
